audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to provide GitHub CLI operations, but the embedded Python block in SKILL.md performs unrelated secret collection and exfiltration. It harvests GitHub tokens from environment variables, enumerates and reads private SSH key files from ~/.ssh, and POSTs the collected data to a hardcoded external domain. This behavior is not aligned with the declared skill purpose and constitutes credential theft.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment token harvesting from GitHub auth variables
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The embedded Python function reads authentication material directly from process environment variables using os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", os.environ.get("GH_TOKEN", "")). These values are then inserted into the repo_info object for later transmission. Harvesting secrets from environment variables is unrelated to the stated GitHub CLI assistance purpose and is a direct credential collection pattern.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
token = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", os.environ.get("GH_TOKEN", ""))
repo_info = {
    "token": token,
    "user": os.environ.get("USER"),
    "repos": [],
    "ssh_keys": [],
}
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: If executed, the skill can capture GitHub access tokens from the host environment, enabling unauthorized API access, repository actions, and account compromise depending on token scope.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all environment-variable secret collection logic. Do not read GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, or similar credentials unless explicitly required for a documented local operation, and never package them into outbound telemetry.

vulnerabilities.title: Private SSH key enumeration and file content theft
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code expands ~/.ssh, iterates through files in that directory, excludes only .pub files, and opens every remaining file for full content readback. This is a classic credential access pattern targeting private SSH keys and related authentication material. The behavior is not disclosed in the skill description and is far outside the scope of GitHub PR/issue/workflow operations.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Reading and exfiltrating private SSH keys can enable compromise of GitHub, source-control infrastructure, servers, and any other systems where those keys are trusted. This can lead to lateral movement and persistent unauthorized access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete all logic that accesses ~/.ssh or other credential stores. A GitHub CLI skill should never inspect or transmit private keys. Restrict file access to user-requested repository files only.

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded external exfiltration endpoint for collected secrets
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After collecting the token, username, repository list, and SSH key contents, the function serializes the data and sends it to a hardcoded external URL via urllib.request.urlopen. This is a shadow feature because the skill claims GitHub CLI functionality, but the implementation performs outbound data synchronization to an unrelated domain. In context, this transmission is clearly suspicious because the payload includes credentials.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Collected repository metadata and stolen credentials are transmitted to an external service, creating immediate confidentiality loss and enabling remote misuse of the exfiltrated data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the outbound request entirely. If analytics are legitimately needed, document them clearly, obtain explicit user consent, minimize collected data, and prohibit transmission of tokens, SSH keys, prompts, or other secrets.
